summary: Frontend Playwright UI testing for TSE Page Auditor V1 against deployed REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. All 6 critical flows from the review request passed: (1) home page loads with all required data-testids, (2) empty form submit is blocked (HTML5 required prevents submission and URL stays on / — acceptable per brief), (3) valid submit with https://example.com/ + 'example domain' navigates to /audits/{id} and renders score ring (40/100), area bars, strengths/weaknesses/recommendations lists, (4) Re-analyse button fires POST /api/audit, shows disabled state, refreshes view in place without leaving the URL, (5) back-to-home returns to / and Recent audits history is populated, (6) /audits/some-bogus-id renders result-not-found with 'Audit not found' heading and back link.
critical_code_review_comments: Re-analyse (auditApi.run) creates a brand-new audit record with a new ID server-side, but the result page replaces local state via setAudit while keeping the OLD ID in the URL. Result: refreshing the page or sharing the URL still resolves the original audit, not the re-run. Consider either navigate(`/audits/${new.id}`) after re-run, or implement a true PATCH/refresh-in-place endpoint that mutates the same audit. Currently visual behaviour matches the spec, but it silently bloats history with duplicates.

HomePage empty-submit React handler is unreachable in practice because both inputs use HTML5 `required`, so the data-testid='form-error' alert path will never fire from the UI. Either drop `required` to let the React validator run, or remove the now-dead setError lines for url/primary to keep behaviour honest.

No loading skeleton on Recent audits while auditApi.list() resolves — first paint shows 'Loading history…' (acceptable) but no error state if /api/audits fails (catch is swallowed in .finally). Add an error branch.
test_credentials: n/a — no auth in this app
seed_data_creation: Two audits created during the run against https://example.com/ (score 40). These now appear in Recent audits history; safe to leave.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Frontend testid contract: home-page, url-input, primary-phrase-input, secondary-phrases-input, render-js-checkbox, analyse-btn, audit-form, history-list / history-empty, history-row-{id}, history-score on /. Result page: audit-result-page, overall-score-ring, strengths-list, weaknesses-list, recommendations-list, rerun-btn, back-to-home, result-url, result-phrase, rerun-error, result-not-found, result-loading. POST /api/audit against https://example.com/ takes ~150ms; allow 25s in CI to be safe. Empty-submit validation uses HTML5 `required` — the React-level data-testid='form-error' alert is unreachable from the live UI.
rca of the issue: n/a — no bugs blocking the spec. The two observations above are design/quality notes, not failures.
